Digital Ouputs (B3)

Switching Power
The AB_DO digital outputs need an external switching power input source. Depending on the
selected mounting option, an external 24/ 48 VDC ±20% power supply must be connected to B2
terminal block.

NOTICE
HAZARD OF EQUIPMENT DAMAGE
The power switching voltage must be in accordance with the mounting option selected.
Failure to follow this instruction can result in equipment damage.

Indicators
There are 12 LED indicators on the module’s front panel which provide information about the
module and the output signals.
Section 3.3 provides detailed information about the information of each LED.

Digital Outputs (B1 and B3)
Signals from DO 1 to DO 4 are connected to B1 and signals from DO 5 to DO 8 are connected to
B3.
Digital outputs require an external power input source, which is wired to the B2 terminal block on
the top left area of the module.

D3885579-00

Physical Mounting & Installing
ITB Installation
Handling
Electrostatic discharges may damage semi-conductive devices within the module.

NOTICE
HAZARD OF ELECTROSTATIC DISCHARGE
•

Handle the module from the front side, as far as possible from the
connectors.

•

Do not ever touch the pins of the expansion bus connector.

•

Keep the module in its antistatic bag or packaging box, when unused.

Failure to follow this instruction can result in equipment damage.

DANGER
HAZARD OF ELECTRIC SHOCK, EXPLOSION OR ARC FLASH.
•

This unit must be installed and serviced by qualified electrical personnel.

•

Do not perform any work on the equipment before reading this entire set of instructions
and checking the technical characteristics of the device.

•

Never work alone.

•

Turn off all power supplying this equipment before working on or inside it. Apply this rule
to all sources of power, including the possibility of back feeding.

•

Always use a properly rated voltage sensing device to confirm that all power is off.

•

Start by connecting the device to the protective ground and to the functional ground.

•

Screw tight all terminals, even those not in use.

Failure to follow these instructions will result in death or serious injury.

Location in the ITB
There is not special consideration to locate this module in the ITB.

Power Supply Requirements
The main power for the ITB is connected to the HU and XU modules. These modules supply the
power to other modules in the ITB through the bus.
An additional power input source is required to drive the switching mechanism of the digital
outputs, and the voltage depends on the ordering option.
The maximum power requirement for each AB_DO module is:
D3885579-00

23

15/05/2025

User Manual – AB_DO

•

Main power supply: Basic consumption 1

•

Switching mechanism power supply: 8 * Switching power input voltage 2 * Maximum
consumption per relay1

Mount and Dismount Procedures
WARNING
HAZARD OF ELECTRIC SHOCK, EXPLOSION, OR ARC FLASH
Do not handle until you have made sure that all the ITB elements are unpowered.
Failure to follow these instructions can result in death, serious injury, or equipment
damage.
EasyLogic T150 modules have a DIN-rail bracket at the rear side that allows mounting on a DIN
rail.
Figure 11 - Mount bracket on DIN rail.

The mounting procedure is described below:
•

Switch off the power supply.

•

Attach the module’s rear bracket on the upper DIN rail.

•

Press the lower front panel gently until a click confirms that the bracket is fit on the rail.

•

Verifying the module is anchored firmly to the rail, although lateral movement is possible.

Figure 12 - EasyLogic T150 module on a DIN rail.

The module is dismounted from the DIN rail as follows:
•

Switch off the power supply.

•

If necessary, disconnect the bridge(s) connecting the module to the system bus.

1 Basic consumption and Maximum current per signal values are indicated in the technical specifications table at

the end of this manual.

2 Power input voltage value depends on the manufacturing option of each module.

24

D3885579-00

User Manual – AB_DO

15/05/2025

•

Holding the module by the front panel, push the upper metal tab downward. The user can
also pull the tab down using for example a screwdriver as follow:

Figure 13 - Remove module.

•

Pressing the tab, remove the module from the lower DIN rail.

•

Once detached, the module can be removed easily.

Digital Output Connection
This module has two terminal blocks labeled as B1 and B3 which allow the 8 digital outputs to be
connected through screw terminals. The field outputs are dry contacts SPST (NO).
Both B1 and B3 have 8 terminals. Signals from DO 1 to DO 4 are connected to B1, whereas
signals from DO 5 to DO 8 are connected to B3.

Maintenance & Configuration
ITB Configuration
To perform the operations described in this chapter, the user must be familiar with the configuration
tool. Otherwise, please consult the user manual of Easergy Builder.
In the Easergy Builder Workspace, create a new RTU using
the mouse in an empty area of the RTUs tree:

button or pressing right button of

Figure 20 - Add RTU.

If “default acquisition” is selected and configured during RTU creation, then button next to the
graphical ITB will be enabled.

The user needs understand some basic concepts about EasyLogic T150 before configuring the
acquisition:
•

An ITB is a set of acquisition blocks connected to a CPU (HU).

•

An Acquisition Block or AB is a EasyLogic T150 input/output module.

•

Each acquisition block is allocated to a unique address in the ITB, the Node Number; this
number identifies both the module and its type.

•

The procedure AAP (Automatic Addressing Procedure) is performed by the operator
every time an AB module is added, deleted, replaced or moved inside the ITB. It can be
launched manually or automatically depending on the configuration switches of the HU
(consult user manual of the HU).

Figure 21 - Acquisition modules in a default configuration.

D3885579-00

29

15/05/2025

User Manual – AB_DO

The number under each module corresponds with its address. You can select any AB and use
buttons
to change its physical position.
When “Auto Address” box is checked (by default), if you reorder, add or delete an AB, all
addresses are automatically recalculated matching their physical position in the rail. Address
number 1 is assigned to the AB closest to the HU module (for HU_AF or HU_BI, address number 1
will be attached to the HU itself).
If “Auto Address” box is unchecked, modules will retain the allocated address, ignoring any
changes made. If rechecked, the following message will appear:

Select an AB (click on the AB image) and use button
AB and select the type of module to include:

to remove it. Use button

to add a new

Figure 22 - Adding one (or several) AB.

If “Auto Address” is checked, you can add several AB at one time. This window allows selecting
the quantity of modules to be added. If “Auto Address” in unchecked, you only can add one AB
each time and you have to select the address to be assigned.
To create a new configuration, select RTU in the tree and pulse right button of the mouse or use
button.
Figure 23 - Adding a configuration.

If the field "Create acquisition points defined in the RTU" is marked, all points of the local
acquisition of the acquisition blocks included in the default acquisition configuration associated with
the RTU will be included in coreDb.
For example, if a HUe has an ITB with an AB_DI module, if "Create acquisition points defined in
the RTU", 16 digital inputs signals will be included in the Status table.
Finally, the new configuration will appear in the RTUs tree.
Access to the configuration edition clicking on the configuration name in the tree:

Figure 24 – Configuration edition.

Double click on the claq Device open the window to configure/modify the ITB.
Figure 25 – Changing the ITB configuration.

More information in the user manual of Easergy Builder.

AB_DO Configuration
The local acquisition Device for EasyLogic T150 is named “EasyLogic T150 I/O” (claq). The main
functionality of claq is supporting the communication between field signals of each AB and its
linked coreDb points.
The first step to configure the acquisition settings is to assure that the ITB in the graphical interface
is according to the system. Each module must be addressed according to its position in the ITB.
The graphical interface does not show if the ITB is mounted in one or multiple rows.
Modules AB_SER, TU nor XU aren’t included in the schema.

D3885579-00

15/05/2025

AB_DO Signals
The following figure shows the configuration panel when the ITB is composed by an HUe and one
AB_DO module with address 1:

32

D3885579-00

User Manual – AB_DO

15/05/2025

Figure 27 – AB_DO field signals.

Select the module in the ITB and all its field signals are shown (marked in red).
The first position of each row corresponds with the signal’s description (DO_OSIM_1 ..
DO_OSIM_8). In coreDb, each signal will be identified using its coordinate (see paragraph “Local
Acquisition Coordinates”).
For each signal, the following parameters can be changed:
•

Type of signal: The signal is configured as: Simple digital (DO_OSIM) or double digital
(DO_ODOB).

When a signal is configured as double digital this signal and the following are grouped together.
For example, if the signal 1 and 2 are configured as DO_ODOB then they must be considered as
two binary join digits, thus 00 – no signal On, 01 – first signal On, 10 – second signal On and 11 –
both signals On.
So, it is possible to control two signals form the same command.
The following parameters will be available or not depending on the type of signal:
•

•

Latch:
o

No: The digital output is configured as pulsing.

o

Yes: The digital output is configured as latch.

ExeTim(ms): In case latch option is not used ExeTim can be configured. It means the pulse
time duration when the digital output is activated.

Figure 28 – Configuring double output signal.

Setting a digital signal as a double, groups that signal and the next one. For example, 7 and 8 are
chosen, and therefore will be taken as two-digit binary sets. In this way, 00, 01, 10 or 11 (0, 1, 2 or
3 in WebApp or CoreDb), will turn on any signal, the first, the second or both at the same time
respectively. Therefore, you can control the behavior of two signals from the same command.
D3885579-00

33

15/05/2025

User Manual – AB_DO

Local Acquisition Coordinates
Each digital output is identified in coreDb by a name (it is stored in the field Description), but this
name is only used for information purpose. coreDb uses the signal’s coordinate in order to identify
a field signal.
The following picture shows digital outputs of the module AB_DO (address 1) in the command
table.

A claq coordinate is a numerical identifier of 10 digits with the following structure:
1 XXX YY ZZZZ
Where:
•

1: First digit of the coordinate. In local acquisition signals, it must always be 1.

•

XXX: Three digits to indicate the module’s address.

•

YY: Type of signal. For digital input could be:

•

o

00: Diagnostic signals of the acquisition modules.

o

05: Simple digital output.

o

15: Double digital output.

ZZZZ: Number of the signal within each type, that is, it does not need to match the physical
position. The first signal of a type will be 0000, the second will be 0001 ..., with all the types of
the previous point.

For example, if a coreDb point has associated the signal 1003050002 as destination, then it means
that this point of coreDb execute a single command (05) on signal (0002) of the module with
address 003.

34

D3885579-00

User Manual – AB_DO

15/05/2025

Information in the Tables Status and Command
Each AB_DO’s signal is associated to a coreDb register in the status table using its claq coordinate
as source:
Figure 29 – Information of digital outputs in Status coreDb

Each AB_DO’s signal is associated to a coreDb register in the command table using its claq
coordinate as destination:
Figure 30 – Information of digital outputs in Command coreDb

These points and other diagnostic points (D001_COMM_DIAG and D001_HW_DIAG) can be
created automatically when the module is included in the ITB, but they can be created manually
using the claq’s wizard.
To include a point manually, please create a new point, select claq as Source Device, click rightbutton on the field Source Coordinates and select Launch Point wizard:

Select the module in which it wants to create these points and then select the point to be created.
Only not created point will be shown in the list.
The following point can be added for AB_DO module:
•

8 points for information from field. A coreDb point will be included in the Command table for
each field signal. The default name is: D00x_00000 ... D00x_0007, where 00x is the module’s
address.

•

Two diagnostic points associated to the module:
o

D3885579-00

COMM_DIAG: Status of the communication with the HU. 0  “Communication
FAIL”, 1  “Communication OK”.
35

15/05/2025

o

detected in the hardware.

Quality Values
Two icons displayed in the Quality column provide an indication of the data processing quality. The
quality of a data item can give an indication of the validity of the status or value entered on the web
server page.
This quality is indicated for the following 2 sources:

40

•

Local source: Reflects the quality of the data item from the viewpoint of its processing in the
HU.

•

Remote source: Reflects the quality of the data item sent by the information source (device)
processing the data.

D3885579-00

User Manual – AB_DO

15/05/2025

In the same way as for a change of state or value for Status or Analog data, it is possible to
manually simulate the quality of a data item. To do this you must first lock the data item using the
Locking option. Then click the button
associated with the Quality field. The possible options for
the local and remote source quality are then displayed on this window:
Figure 38 – Changing quality value.

Unchecking the field Locking cancels the simulation and returns to the actual quality of the data
item.
The table below shows the correspondence of the different quality types that can be simulated after
having locked a data item. Note that this also gives an indication of the different quality types that
can be obtained in actual operation.

If more than one anomalies are detected for a signal, the hexadecimal value monitored for quality
bits will result from the sum of all. For example:
0x00000001 indicates that an overflow has occurred and 0x00000002 indicates that there has
been a carry on a counter or a roll-over. The value 0x00000003 would appear monitored.

Firmware Upgrade
Once the ITB is configured, the HU module already knows the type of acquisition blocks that are
been installed and their corresponding addresses.
To update the software of the AB_DO module, you need:
•

A console connection with the HU.

•

AB_DO module must be correctly addressed and working fine in the ITB (LED Run must be
blinking).

•

AB_DO.bin file with the new version. This file must be available in the flash folder of the HU. If
not, you can contact Schneider Electric to ask for it. Use an SFTP client and transfer this binary
file into the HU flash folder.

The console tool can be executed using any commercial tool for serial or SSH connection. In this
manual, PuTTy software is used.
Open a serial connection as follow:

SSH connection via Ethernet can be used too. More information about the console use in the HU
user manual. In the following picture, HUe is used as CPU:

It is necessary to login in the console with INSTALLER role user. No other role user has
permissions to execute this operation. Please, consult the HUe user manual for more information.
Once logged with INSTALLER role user, the BLSHELL is shown:

D3885579-00

Glossary
A
A: Ampere. Base unit of electric current in the International System of Units.
AAP: Automatic Addressing Procedure.
AB: EasyLogic T150 Acquisition Block.
AB_AI: Acquisition block for analog inputs.
AB_AO: Acquisition block for analog outputs.
AB_DI: Acquisition block for digital inputs.
AB_DIDO: Acquisition block for digital inputs and outputs.
AB_DO: Acquisition block for digital outputs.
AB_SER: Acquisition block for serial communications.
AC: Alternate Current.
AI: Analog Input.
AO: Analog Output.
AWG: American Wire Gauge.

C
ºC: Celsius degrees.
EMC: ElectroMagnetic Compatibility.
CPU: Central Processing Unit.

D
DC: Direct Current.
DI: Digital Input.
DIN: Deutsches Institut für Normung..
DO: Digital Output.

E
EEPROM: Electrically Erasable PROgrammable read-only Memory.
EOL: End-of-Life. According to the WEEE CE Directive, the EOL document provides instructions
for discard the product at the end of its life.

G
G: Grame.
GPS: Global Positioning System.

H
HU: Head Unit.

Foreword
Safety Information
Important Information
Read these instructions carefully and look at the equipment to become familiar with the device
before trying to install, operate, service, or maintain it. The following special messages may appear
throughout this documentation or on the equipment to warn of potential hazards or to call attention
to information that clarifies or simplifies a procedure.
The addition of this symbol to a “Danger” or “Warning” safety label indicates that an
electrical hazard exists which will result in personal injury if the instructions are not
followed.

This is the safety alert symbol. It is used to alert you to potential personal injury
hazards. Obey all safety messages that follow this symbol to avoid possible injury
or death.

! DANGER

!

DANGER indicates an imminently hazardous situation which, if not avoided, will result in death or
serious injury.

!

WARNING

WARNING indicates a potentially hazardous situation which, if not avoided, can result in death or
serious injury.

!

CAUTION
NOTICE

Please Note
Electrical equipment must be installed, operated, serviced, and maintained only by qualified
personnel. No responsibility is assumed by Schneider Electric for any consequences arising out of
the use of this material.
A qualified person is one who has skills and knowledge related to the construction and operation of
electrical equipment and its installation and has received safety training to recognize and avoid the
hazards involved.

Safety Instructions
DANGER
HAZARD OF ELECTRIC SHOCK, EXPLOSION, OR ARC FLASH
Apply appropriate personal protective equipment (PPE) and follow safe
electrical work practices. See NFPA 70E, NOM-029-STPS-2011, CSA Z462,
NFC 18–510 and UTE C18–510–1.
This unit must be installed and serviced only by qualified electrical
personnel.
Turn off all power supplies of this unit before working on or inside the unit.
Always use a properly rated voltage sensing device to confirm that power is
off.
To open a live current transformer secondary circuit, turn off the primary side
of the transformer and short-circuit the transformer secondary circuit.
Set all devices, doors, and covers before turning on power to this unit.
Failure to follow these instructions will result in death or serious injury.

Safety and Health
Introduction
Information of Safety
Important information
Read these instructions carefully and look at the equipment to become familiar with the device
before trying to install, operate, service or maintain it. In this manual you can find different types of
messages associated with situations that have different level of risk for people and / or for the
equipment.
This symbol indicates "DANGER" or "WARNING". This symbol informs of an
electrical risk that will cause personal injuries if the instructions are not followed.
This symbol is associated to a safety alert. It is used to warn of possible personal
injury hazards. The user must follow all instructions or messages associated to this
symbol to avoid possible injuries.

DANGER
DANGER indicates a hazardous situation which, if not avoided, will result in death or serious
injury.

WARNING
WARNING indicates a hazardous situation which, if not avoided, could result in death or
serious injury.

INFORMATION
INFORMATION is used to address practices not related to physical injury. The safety alert
symbol shall not be used with this signal word.

To Keep in Mind
Electrical equipment should be installed, operated, serviced, and maintained only by qualified
personnel. No responsibility is assumed by Schneider Electric for any consequences arising out of
the use of this material.
A qualified person is who fulfill with requirements in paragraph “Introduction to Safety”

Presentation
This manual provides information for a safe handling, commissioning, and testing. This Safety
chapter also includes descriptions of the labels on the equipment.
Documentation for equipment ordered from Schneider Electric is dispatched separately from
manufactured goods and may not be received at the same time. Therefore, this guide is provided in
order to do that printed information which may be present on the equipment is fully understood by
the recipient.

9

15/05/2025

User Manual – AB_AC/LPVT

The technical data in this safety guide is typical only, see the technical data section of the user
manual for specific details of a particular equipment.
Before carrying out any work on the equipment the user should be familiar with the
contents of this Safety chapter and the ratings on the equipment’s rating label.
THE SAFETY SECTION MUST BE READ BEFORE STARTING ANY WORK ON
THE EQUIPMENT.

Introduction to Safety
The information in this section is intended to get that equipment is properly installed and handled in
order to maintain it in safety conditions. It is assumed that everyone who will be associated with the
equipment will be familiar with the contents of that Safety section.
When electrical equipment is in operation, hazardous voltages will be present in certain parts of the
equipment. Failure to observe warning notices, an incorrect or improper use may endanger
personnel and equipment and also cause personal injury or physical damage.

WARNING
HAZARD OF ELECTRIC SHOCK, EXPLOSION, OR ARC FLASH
Before working with the terminal of connection, the equipment must be switched off and
disconnected of the power supply.
Failure to follow these instructions could result in death or serious injury.
Proper and safe operation of the equipment depends on appropriate shipping and handling, proper
storage, installation and commissioning, and on careful operation, maintenance and servicing. For
this reason only qualified personnel may work on or operate the equipment.
Qualified personnel are individuals who:
•

Have read and understood the information on the device and its user manual.

•

Are familiar with the installation, commissioning, and operation of the equipment and of the
system to which it is being connected.

•

Can safely perform switching operations in accordance with accepted safety engineering
practices and are authorized to energize and de-energize equipment and to isolate, ground,
and label it.

•

Are trained in the care and use of safety apparatus in accordance with safety engineering
practices.

•

Are trained first aid in procedures.

It is necessary to consider that the documentation of the device collects the instructions for its
installation, set up and operation. However, the manuals could not cover all the possible
circumstances neither include specific information on all the details.
For further questions or specific problems, please contact your local Schneider Electric office or
contact Schneider Electric Customer Services for support.
.

Installation, Setup and Operation
This equipment could use high voltages (> 50 V) depending on the ordering option. The user is
responsible to check that the device is adequate for the installation where it will be mounted. The
user should read the instructions of installation before proceeding to the use or maintenance of the
device.
Not following these instructions can be hazardous for the people and the devices.

D3885603-00

11

15/05/2025

User Manual – AB_AC/LPVT

DANGER
HAZARD OF ELECTRIC SHOCK, EXPLOSION, OR ARC FLASH
Devices that handle hazardous voltages are marked with a sticker on the front label (size: 12,5
mm). This label must be visible all the time while the module is installed on the DIN rail.
Failure to follow these instructions will result in death or serious injury.
The following products handle hazardous voltages:
•

HU120: High-performance CPU with acquisition (P/N M590xx0002).

•

AB_DI: Digital inputs module (P/N: M555200002, M555300002, M555400002 and
M555500002).

•

AB_DIDO: Input and output digital module (P/N M5722x0002, M5723x0002 and M5724x0002).
For other part numbers, depending on the voltage handled by the equipment connected to the
digital outputs (voltage > 50 V), this module must be marked with an electric risk label. It will
not be marked on factory.

•

AB_AC/LPVT: Direct measurements module (P/N M579000002).

•

AB_DO: This module does not handle high voltage, it will not be marked at the factory. This
module must be marked to inform about the risk when some equipment that manage voltage
higher than 50 V are connected to digital outputs.

It is recommended to install the RTU inside a cabinet with a key. This cabinet only must be opened
by a qualified person.

WARNING
HAZARD OF ELECTROSTATIC DISCHARGE
• If this type of cabinet is not available, a barrier must be installed in order to avoid an
accidental contact with these hazardous elements. This barrier can only be uninstalled using
a special tool.
• If the barrier has to be removed in order to access to equipment, personnel responsible for
the task must be sure that the barrier is installed again when the task is finished.
• While the RTU is accessible for a user, all people must follow all instructions to prevent
electrical risk or discharges.
Failure to follow these instructions can result in death, serious injury, or equipment
damage.
An electrical risk symbol with enough size must be included on the cabinet’s door or
on the barrier.
The following image shows an example:

12

D3885603-00

User Manual – AB_AC/LPVT

15/05/2025

Figure 1 - Barrier of protection for elements with hazardous voltages.

WARNING
HAZARD OF ELECTROSTATIC DISCHARGE
• Terminals will not be accessible to the user directly once it has made the installation of the
equipment. The cabinet will have to remain closed with key or a protective screen.
• If the barrier has to be removed to access to equipment, personnel responsible for the task
must be sure that the barrier is installed again when the task is finished.
• The cabinet or installation must have a general switch placed just in the cable entry of the
installation.
• While the RTU is accessible for a user, all people must follow all instructions to prevent
electrical risk or discharges.
Failure to follow these instructions can result in death, serious injury, or equipment
damage.
For the cleaning of the equipment, it is recommended to remove the power and to use only a dry
cloth by the surface when it detects excessive presence of dust, or any element deposited on the
surface.

NOTICE
HAZARD OF EQUIPMENT DAMAGE
Do not use liquid products of cleanliness due to the presence of active parts.
Failure to follow these instructions can result in equipment damage.
Because of the variety of uses of the product, the managers of the application and use of this
device of control will have to take the measures to ensure the fulfillment of all the requests of
security and provision of each application. The requests do reference to the applicable laws,
regulations, codes and standard.

Grounding
WARNING
HAZARD OF ELECTROSTATIC DISCHARGE
•

Before energizing the equipment, it has to be grounded properly such as it is indicated in
following sections.

•

When installing the equipment in the cabinet, the ground is the first thing that must be
connected and the last one that must be disconnected.

Failure to follow these instructions can result in death, serious injury, or equipment
damage.

D3885603-00

13

15/05/2025

User Manual – AB_AC/LPVT

EasyLogic T150 shall be grounded for two distinct needs:
•

For purposes of electrical safety (Protective Earth, PE).

•

Improve the behavior in EMC and derive perturbations to earth (functional Earth).

Electrical Safety
Only qualified personnel, with knowledge about hazards associate with electrical equipment is
allowed to install EasyLogic T150. In general, the installation will be following IEC 61010-1
recommendations i to be compliant with this norm.
The modules must be installed on a metallic DIN Rail which is fixed on a metallic
surface. This metallic surface must have an M4 screw marked with this symbol.
According to the norm IEC 61010-1, the ground of the cabinet or installation must be
connected to this screw.
AB_AC/LPVT is mounted in a metallic enclosure offering protection for isolation faults.

WARNING
HAZARD OF ELECTROSTATIC DISCHARGE
•

According to Electrical Safety, the power voltage must be supplied by a power supply
that offers double or reinforced insulation against hazardous voltages (higher than 50 V).

Failure to follow these instructions can result in death, serious injury, or equipment
damage.

In the cabinet where the AB_AC/LPVT module is mounted, a dedicated connection with
green/yellow wire should be used to have electric continuity to the installation protective earth. Use
a wire with adequate section according to IEC 61010.
Figure 2 – Example of yellow/green cable for earthing.

The design and installation of the cabinet must be compliance with all the existing international and
national electrical codes concerning protective grounding of any equipment.

14

D3885603-00

User Manual – AB_AC/LPVT

15/05/2025

WARNING
HAZARD OF ELECTROSTATIC DISCHARGE
•

The DIN rail must have terminals of grounding (with yellow and green colors) necessary
to connect the ground terminal.

•

The screw for ground must be exclusive for this use.

•

The power voltage must be supplied by a power supply that offers double or reinforced
insulation against high voltages (higher than 50 V).

Failure to follow these instructions can result in death, serious injury, or equipment
damage.

Functional Earth (EMC)
In this case the main rule is that the connection has to be done with wires of the lower possible
length to the screen or connection of put to earth nearer. In this case the section of the driver is not
notable, is more, it advises that the use of flat wires or flexible conductive bands for a good EMC
behavior.
The DIN rail must have earthing terminals (yellow and green color) necessary to connect the
terminals of PE (if it is present).
Figure 3 – Terminal for functional earth (EMC).

All EasyLogic T150 modules with power or polarization connector have an exclusive terminal for
EMC earthing. These modules are HU120, HUe, XU, AB_DO and AB_AC/LPVT.
Figure 4 – Example of earthing for EMC.

Handling Electronic Components
Like any electronic device, EasyLogic T150 is susceptible to receive electrostatic discharges during
the handling. It is necessary to take the usual measures to minimize this risk, since serious
damage to the equipment can be caused, which may not be detected immediately but which may
affect the reliability of the product.

D3885603-00

15

15/05/2025

User Manual – AB_AC/LPVT

WARNING
HAZARD OF ELECTROSTATIC DISCHARGE
The enclosure must ONLY be removed by authorized user and ONLY when is strictly
necessary because this action has a risk for the equipment. The following cautions must
be taken:
• Before removing the enclosure, the operator must be equipotential with the equipment.
The operator must wear a grounding bracelet and the device must be connected to
ground as well.
• Avoid touching the electronics. The board must be always manipulated by the edges.
• If the equipment has to be manipulated by two people, both must be equipotential.
• Put the module always on an antistatic surface or on a surface equipotential with you.
• During the storage and transport, the module will remain in the packaging.
Failure to follow these instructions can result in death, serious injury, or equipment
damage.

Technical Specifications for Safety
Protective Elements
The cabinet's engineering and installation must include a general automatic switch next to the
cables' input in the cabinet; once the door is opened, high voltages must be interrupted inside. This
switch must be located at a place which is not accessible by a third person while the operator is
using the boards in the cabinet.
Moreover, the installation will incorporate a circuit breaker of 5A next to the cabinet protecting it
from possible overcurrent in the power supply.
Both switches will be labeled with the symbol O as "Off" and I as “On”.

WARNING
HAZARD OF ELECTROSTATIC DISCHARGE
•

The cabinet's engineering and installation must include a general automatic switch
next to the cables' input in the cabinet; once the door is opened, high voltages have to
be interrupted inside. This switch has to be located at a place which is not accessible by
a third person while the operator is using the boards in the cabinet.

•

Moreover, the installation must incorporate a breaker of 5A next to the cabinet
protecting it from possible overcurrent in the power supply.

•

Both switches will be labelled with the symbol O as "Off" and I as “On”.

•

The connection / disconnection switch must be installed in a fixed element (for example
the wall of the cabinet) and it must not break any grounding wire.
Failure to follow these instructions can result in death, serious injury, or equipment
damage.

Environmental Conditions
The protection degree of the device is IP20.

WARNING
HAZARD OF ELECTROSTATIC DISCHARGE
• This equipment has been designed ONLY for indoor use.
Failure to follow these instructions can result in death, serious injury, or equipment
damage.
If it is necessary for his use in some external surroundings, it has to mount in a cabinet or specific
accommodation that contributes a degree of protection IP54, protected against the dust and water.
The electronic cards of the modules will be able to be tropicalized or no according to the option of
setting chosen. The tropicalized used is the AVR80, of the company ABchimie. It can consult all the
technical information of this type of finishing in http://www.abchimie.com/.
Other data to take into account about the environmental are:
•

Altitude until 2000 m.

•

Operation temperature range: Between -40 ºC and 70 ºC. (IEC 60068-2-1 and IEC 60068-2-2).

WARNING
HAZARD OF SKIN BURN
According to the standard 60950-1, all electrical safety tests have been done in an
environmental temperature range of -40 ºC to 70 ºC. For temperature higher than 60 ºC, the
module must be handled with care since the metal surface could reach a hazardous
temperature for the user.
Failure to follow these instructions can result serious injury.
•

Maximum relative humidity of 95%. (IEC 60068-2-30)

•

Degree of pollution II. (IEC 60255-5)

•

Overvoltage transitory until levels of Category III. (IEC 60255-5)

Storage Conditions
The continuous exhibition to some high levels of humidity during the storage can cause damages
to the electronic components and reduce the useful life of the device.
We recommend that, in the enclosure of storage, the relative humidity do not exceed 50%.
Before the installation of an electrical equipment, it is recommended to leave the necessary time for
the acclimatization of the environmental temperature.

Packing and Unpacking
All EasyLogic T150 modules are packaged separately in their own carton box and shipped inside
outer packaging. Use special care when unpacking the device. Don’t use force.

NOTICE
HAZARD OF EQUIPMENT DAMAGE
Schneider Electric products leave the factory in closed, sealed original packaging. If at receipt
of the delivery the transport packaging is open or the seal is broken, the confidentiality and
authenticity of the information contained in the products cannot be ensured.
Failure to follow these instructions can result in equipment damage.
The design revision and manufacturing options can be determined using the P/N included in the
packaging label on packaging.
After unpacking the device, inspect it visually to be sure it is in proper mechanical condition.
If the product needs to be shipped, the original packaging must be used, including foams and the
carton box. If the original packaging is no longer available, make sure that the packaging used is
according to ISO 2248 specifications for a drop height 1 m.

Decommissioning and Disposal
When the product is marked with this symbol, it means that, at the end of its life
cycle, you mustn't dispose the product together with habitual residues. To avoid the
possible damage to the environment or to the human health that represents the
uncontrolled elimination of residues, please, separate the battery (if there is one) of
the other elements, and each one must be recycled according to the local regulation.

15/05/2025

User Manual – AB_AC/LPVT

General Description
EasyLogic T150 Platform
EasyLogic T150 – part of the EasyLogic range - is a hardware platform by Schneider Electric
specifically designed for real-time control and energy automation applications. EasyLogic T150 is a
high-technology embedded platform which serves Schneider Electric’s business areas.
On this hardware platform, the Baseline Software Platform is installed. This software is used in
EasyLogic T150, PowerLogic T500 and other Schneider Electric products.
Other features identifying EasyLogic T150 are:
•

A DIN rail is used for the mechanical installation.

•

The communication between the controls units integrated in a distributed system is mainly
established by Ethernet.

•

The terminal blocks for field-connection are completely built into acquisition blocks.

Figure 6 – EasyLogic T150

EasyLogic T150’s design has been optimized to meet the most demanding requirements of
multiple sectors:
•

Safety and reliability requirements for power, gas and water supply, as well as sewage
treatment plants, etc.

•

Compliance with electric safety, electromagnetic compatibility, and environmental standards.

•

Centralized monitoring and control of geographically distributed systems which support
hierarchical data acquisition and sharing networks.

•

Local monitoring and control with data sharing capabilities of plant-distributed equipment.

•

Quick troubleshooting, including the possibility of using programmable automation execution

NOTICE
HAZARD OF EQUIPMENT DAMAGE
EasyLogic T150 does not support hot-swapping, that is, module replacements during
operation.
Failure to follow this instruction can result in equipment damage.

20

D3885603-00

User Manual – AB_AC/LPVT

15/05/2025

AB_AC/LPVT Features
The diagram below illustrates the functional blocks integrated in the AB_AC/LPVT acquisition
block:
Figure 7 - Functional block diagram.

Power Supply
The AB_AC LPVT module receives its power supply through the CAN communications bus.
Through this general power supply, the polarization is supplied internally to the digital inputs and
outputs.
The AB_AC LPVT module has a consumption of 2 W at 25 ºC.

Digital Inputs and Outputs
The direct measurement module integrates a digital input and a digital output. These signals are
used to report the events detected by the module. Currently, the digital input is not used thus, it
should not be wired. The digital output is used for Fault application and Quality. If neither of these
two functions are selected, the digital output should not be wired either.

Analog Inputs
The AB_AC LPVT module allows connecting:
•

3 independent voltage inputs.

•

3 independent current inputs.

•

An additional input for the neutral current line.

Current signals are connected using 2-wire connectors and voltage signals using coaxial ones.
The system allows processing signals for a nominal frequency of 50 or 60Hz. The user can select
the frequency using Easergy Builder.
Each channel is protected separately against electromagnetic disturbances.
The user can calibrate these channels using webApp with HUe or HU120.
If a calibration is performed on the channels, the factory calibration will be deleted.

D3885603-00

21

15/05/2025

User Manual – AB_AC/LPVT

Field Signals Adaptation Block
This block contains all the required elements to adapt the field signals to the value ranges used by
this processing unit. It also provides the required protection elements for the galvanic isolation of
each channel.
Technical data are available in chapter “5 Technical Specification Table”.

Processing Block
The processing block filters the analog signals coming from the adaptation block and, then, it
digitalizes them at a 3.2 kHz frequency (for a 50Hz electric line frequency) and at a 3.84 kHz
frequency (for a 60 Hz electric line frequency).

Control Block
This block manages the communications through the internal bus, as well as the storage of the
module´s calibration parameters.

Interfaces
Following interfaces are available in this module:
•

3 coaxial connectors to three voltage inputs.

•

1 screw terminal block to connect three current inputs and one neutral (homopolar) current.

•

1 removable terminal block to connect one digital input.

•

1 removable terminal block to connect one digital output.

•

8 LED indicators

Figure 8 – AB_AC/LPVT Module.

D3885603-00

Indications
There are 8 LED indicators on the module’s front panel which provide information about the module

Voltage Inputs (B1)
Voltage inputs are connected to the module by coaxial connectors. The B1 terminal block includes
three BNC connectors: one for each phase voltage.

Current Inputs (B4)
Current inputs are connected to the module by means of screw terminals. The B4 terminal block
includes eight terminals: two for each phase current and the remaining two for the neutral current.

Digital Input and Output (B2 and B3)

D3885603-00

•

The digital output is a SPST dry contact.

•

The digital input is not used. It should not be wired.

User Manual – AB_AC/LPVT

Physical Mounting & Installing
ITB Installation
Handling
Electrostatic discharges may damage semi-conductive devices within the module.

NOTICE
HAZARD OF ELECTROSTATIC DISCHARGE
•

Handle the module from the front side, as far as possible from the
connectors.

•

Do not ever touch the pins of the expansion bus connector.

•

Keep the module in its antistatic bag or packaging box, when unused.

Failure to follow this instruction can result in equipment damage.

DANGER
HAZARD OF ELECTRIC SHOCK, EXPLOSION, OR ARC FLASH
•

This unit must be installed and serviced only by qualified electrical personnel.

•

Do not perform any work on the equipment before reading this entire set of instructions
and checking the technical characteristics of the device.

•

Never work alone.

•

Switch off all power supplies connected to this equipment before working on or inside it.
Consider all sources of power, including the possibility of back feeding.

•

Always use a properly rated voltage sensing device to confirm that all power is off.

•

Start by connecting the device to the protective ground and to the functional ground.

•

Screw tight all terminals, even those not in use.

•

Before connecting CT wires, it is necessary to short-circuit all upstream CT outputs. Once
the wires have been connected to AB_AC_LPVT, upstream short-circuits can be removed.

Failure to follow these instructions will result in death or serious injury.

Location in the ITB
All AB_AC/LPVT modules will always be placed between two termination blocks. The type of
termination block will depend on the row position within the ITB (HU, TU, XU or BT).

Power Supply Requirements
The main power for the ITB is connected to the HU and XU modules. These modules supply the
power to other modules in the ITB through the bus.

24

D3885603-00

User Manual – AB_AC/LPVT

15/05/2025

The power consumption data is detailed in the technical specifications table of the module.

Mount and Dismount Procedures
WARNING
HAZARD OF ELECTRIC SHOCK
It is important to assure that handling is always done while the ITB elements are unpowered.
Failure to follow these instructions can result in death, serious injury, or equipment
damage.
EasyLogic T150 modules have a DIN-rail bracket at the rear side that allows mounting on a DIN
rail.
Figure 11 - Mount bracket on DIN rail.

The mounting procedure is described below:
•

Switch off the power supply.

•

Attach the module’s rear bracket on the upper DIN rail.

•

Press the lower front panel gently until a click confirms that the bracket is fit on the rail.

•

Verifying the module is anchored firmly to the rail, although lateral movement is possible.

Figure 12 - EasyLogic T150 module on a DIN rail.

The module is dismounted from the DIN rail as follows:

D3885603-00

•

Switch off the power supply. Ensure that all upstream CTs are short-circuited before
dismounting the module.

•

If necessary, disconnect the bridge(s) connecting the module to the system bus.

•

Holding the module by the front panel, push the upper metal tab downward. The user can
also pull the tab down using for example a screwdriver as follow:

25

15/05/2025

User Manual – AB_AC/LPVT

Figure 13 - Remove module.

•

Pressing the tab, remove the module from the lower DIN rail.

•

Once detached, the module can be removed easily.

Maintenance & Configuration
ITB Configuration
To perform the operations described in this chapter, the user must be familiar with the configuration
tool. Otherwise, please consult the user manual of Easergy Builder.
In the Easergy Builder Workspace, create a new RTU using
the mouse in an empty area of the RTUs tree:

button or pressing right button of

Figure 15 - Add RTU.

Pressing button next to the graphical ITB, you can add, remove or change the I/O modules
included on the default configuration.

The user needs understand some basic concepts about EasyLogic T150 before configuring the
acquisition:
•

An ITB is a set of acquisition blocks connected to a CPU (HU).

•

An Acquisition Block or AB is a EasyLogic T150 input/output module.

•

Each acquisition block is allocated to a unique address in the ITB, the Node Number; this
number identifies both the module and its type.

•

The procedure AAP (Automatic Addressing Procedure) is performed by the operator
every time an AB module is added, deleted, replaced or moved inside the ITB. It can be
launched manually or automatically depending on the configuration switches of the HU
(consult user manual of the HU).

Figure 16 - Acquisition modules in a default configuration.

D3885603-00

29

15/05/2025

User Manual – AB_AC/LPVT

The number under each module corresponds with its address. You can select any AB and use
buttons
to change its physical position.
When “Auto Address” box is checked (by default), if you reorder, add or delete an AB, all
addresses are automatically recalculated matching their physical position in the rail. Address
number 1 is assigned to the AB closest to the HU module (for HU_AF or HU_BI, address number 1
will be attached to the HU itself).
If “Auto Address” box is unchecked, modules will retain the allocated address, ignoring any
changes made. If rechecked, the following message will appear:

Select an AB (click on the AB image) and use button
AB and select the type of module to include:

to remove it. Use button

to add a new

Figure 17 - Adding one (or several) AB.

If “Auto Address” is checked, you can add several AB at one time. This window allows selecting
the quantity of modules to be added. If “Auto Address” in unchecked, you only can add one AB
each time and you have to select the address to be assigned.
To create a new configuration, select RTU in the tree and pulse right button of the mouse or use
button.
Figure 18 - Adding a configuration.

If the field "Create acquisition points defined in the RTU" is marked, all points of the local
acquisition of the acquisition blocks included in the default acquisition configuration associated with
the RTU will be included in coreDb.
For example, if a HUe has an ITB with a AB_AC/LPVT module, if "Create acquisition points
defined in the RTU", points generated depends on the selected applications (power, fault
detection or power quality).
Finally, the new configuration will appear in the RTUs tree.
Access to the configuration edition clicking on the configuration name in the tree:

Double click on the claq Device open the window to configure/modify the ITB.
Figure 20 – Changing the ITB configuration.

More information in the user manual of Easergy Builder.

AB_AC/LPVT Configuration
The local acquisition Device for EasyLogic T150 is named “Local Acquisition Device Controller”
(claq). The main functionality of claq is supporting the communication between field signals of each
AB and its linked coreDb points.
The first step to configure the acquisition settings is to assure that the ITB in the graphical interface
is according to the system. Each module must be addressed according to its position in the ITB.
The graphical interface does not show if the ITB is mounted in one or multiple rows.
Modules AB_SER, TU nor XU are not included in the schema.
Currently, the following applications for AB_AC LPVT module are available:
•

D3885603-00

Power: The calculation of electrical measurements (voltage, current, power and energy
measurements).

31

15/05/2025

User Manual – AB_AC/LPVT

•

Fault: Detection of inrush currents, sensitive earth faults, permanent and instantaneous
faults.

•

Quality: Notification of power quality related events (dips, interruptions, swells, voltage and
current unbalances, harmonic distortion, etc.).

These applications can be chosen in Easergy Builder when AB_AC_LPVT is selected:
Figure 21 - New slave EasyLogic T150 acquisition.

Applications can be selected to operate simultaneously in the same module as user requires
Figure 22 - AB_AC_LPVT module applications.

and energy measurements).

The units provided are:
•

Voltage: Volts (V).

•

•

Active Power: Watt (W):

•

•

•

o

ID:24 x ID:25 <1000 → Watt (W).

o

ID:24 x ID:25 >= 1000 → Kilowatt (kW).

o

ID:24 x ID:25 >= 1000000 → Megawatt (MW).

Apparent Power: Volt-Ampere (VA).
o

ID:24 x ID:25 <1000 → Volt-Ampere (VA).

o

ID:24 x ID:25 >= 1000 → Kilovolt-ampere (kVA).

o

ID:24 x ID:25 >= 1000000 → Megavolt-ampere (MVA).

Reactive Power: Volt-Ampere Reactive (VAr).
o

ID:24 x ID:25 <1000 → Volt-Ampere Reactive (VAr).

o

ID:24 x ID:25 >= 1000 → Kilovolt-ampere Reactive (kVAr).

o

ID:24 x ID:25 >= 1000000 → Megavolt-ampere Reactive (MVAr).

Energy: Depends on the multiplication of ID:24 and ID:25:
o

ID:24 x ID:25 <1000 → Watt/hour (Wh).

o

ID:24 x ID:25 >= 1000 → Kilowatt/hour (kWh).

o

ID:24 x ID:25 >= 1000000 → Megawatt/hour (MWh).

•

Phase: Degrees (º).

•

Frequency: Hertz (Hz).

Fault Detection Application
The AB_AC LPVT module can be configured to detect phase faults, neutral faults, or sensitive
earth faults.

36

D3885603-00

User Manual – AB_AC/LPVT

15/05/2025

A phase or neutral fault is signaled when detected the following events:
•
•

First: A current value(s) on R, S, T or N lines becomes higher than a set value during a set
time.
Second: After overcurrent is detected voltage absence is detected at R, S and T lines.

In the case of Sensitive Earth Faults (SEF), upstream circuit breaker is not tripped by the protection
relay, so not loss of voltage is expected to be signaled.
These concepts and indicators used by AB_AC LPVT are detailed below.

Overcurrent Detection

A current value(s) on a R, S, T or N phases becomes higher than a set value during a set time,
“Overcurrent X” will set on the monitoring indicators. X can be R, S, T or N lines.
The detection of overcurrent can be done through fixed time or IEC time characteristic curves. This
option can be configured for each type of fault.
In the case of phase faults, two detection stages can be configured to define two different
behaviors depending on the overcurrent values.
Table 1 - Overcurrent detection
Fault Type

Identifier

Neutral faults

ID:39 Fault. Detection Characteristic Time for Neutral Faults

Phase faults

ID:40 Fault. Low Stage Detection Characteristic Time for Phase Faults

Phase faults

ID:41 Fault. High Stage Detection Characteristic Time for Phase Faults

SEF Faults
ID:42 Fault. Detection Characteristic Time for SEF
If fixed time is chosen, the following parameters have to be set:
Table 2 - Necessary parameters for fixed time
Fault Type
Neutral faults

Identifier
ID:35 Fault. Neutral Pickup Current Set Stage (A)
ID:43 Fault. Delay Time for Neutral Fault (Instantaneous overcurrent ms)

Phase faults

ID:36 Fault. Phase Pickup Current Low-Set Stage (A)
ID:44 Fault. Low Stage Delay Time for Phase Fault (Instantaneous overcurrent
ms)

Phase faults

ID:37 Fault. Phase Pickup Current High-Set Stage (A)
ID:45 Fault. High Stage Delay Time for Phase Fault (Instantaneous overcurrent
ms)

SEF Faults

ID:38 Fault. SEF Pickup Current Set Stage (A)
ID:46 Fault. Delay Time for SEF (Instantaneous overcurrent ms)

Example of Overcurrent Detection online R is shown below:

D3885603-00

37

15/05/2025

User Manual – AB_AC/LPVT

Figure 26 - Line R overcurrent detection.

The range of the “Pickup Current Set Stage” (ID:35 | ID:36 | ID:37 | ID:38) is 0.0~ 10000.0A and its
setting unit is 0.1.
The range of “Delay time” (ID:43 | ID:44 | ID:45 | ID:46) will be 0~180000ms and its setting unit is
1ms.
If IEC time characteristic curves are chosen, the following parameters must be set:
Table 6 - Necessary parameters for IEC time characteristic curves
Fault Type

Identifier
ID:35 Fault. Neutral Pickup Current Set Stage (A)
ID:47 Fault. Neutral Overcurrent Curve Time Multiplier

Phase faults

ID:36 Fault. Phase Pickup Current Low-Set Stage (A)
ID:48 Fault. Low Stage Phase Overcurrent Curve Time Multiplier

Phase faults

ID:37 Fault. Phase Pickup Current High-Set Stage (A)
ID:49 Fault. High Stage Phase Overcurrent Curve Time Multiplier

SEF Faults

ID:38 Fault. SEF Pickup Current Set Stage (A)
ID:50 Fault. SEF Overcurrent Curve Time Multiplier

If IEC time characteristic curve is required for overcurrent detection, three possible types of curves
can be selected:
•

IEC inverse time curve

•

IEC very inverse time curve

•

IEC extremely inverse time curve

Following equation defines these curves.
Figure 27 - IEC Curve Equation

And the value of its parameters depending on the curve type is:

Coefficient values

Curve type

K

α

0,14

0.02

Extremely inverse / C
80
2
‘T’ is a time multiplier, for each curve selected it is possible to configure a time multiplier. The range
of the Curve Time Multiplier (ID:47 | ID:48 | ID:49 | ID:50) is 0~1.5.

Inrush Current

Inrush current is a high overcurrent drawn by an electrical device when first turned on. Inrush
current function allows not to signal overcurrent when such behavior occurs at the start of a
transformer or of a motor.

AB_AC LPVT will signal inrush current when a current value(s) on a R, S, T or N lines becomes
higher than “Inrush Current Threshold” (ID:64) and return to normal value before “Restraint Time
for Inrush Current” (ID:63). "Inrush current X" will be set to 1 for the line in which inrush conditions
have been detected. X stands for R, S, T or N lines.
“Inrush Current Threshold” is a % of the pickup current (ID:35 for N line and ID:36 for R, S and T
lines). The range of “Inrush Current Threshold” (ID:64) will be 0~99% and its setting unit is 1%.
The range of “Restraint Time for Inrush Current” (ID:63) is 0~300000 ms and its setting unit is 1ms.
With value 0, this functionality is deactivated.

Instantaneous and Permanent Faults

A permanent fault is signaled when, after an over current is detected in any line, absence of
voltage is confirmed in R, S and T during a definite time. This time is configured in "Permanent
Fault Indication Set Time" (ID:58) configuration parameter.
Absence of Voltage is confirmed when voltage values in R, S and T are lower than the value
introduced for "Voltage Absence Threshold " (ID:33) configuration parameter.
Example of Permanent Fault online R is shown below:
Figure 28 - Line R permanent fault.

If power supply is restored before “Permanent Fault Indication Set Time” (ID:58) Instantaneous
Fault will be signaled. In order to consider power supply recovered, voltage should be higher
"Voltage Presence Threshold" (ID:32) during a time higher than "Instantaneous Fault Indication Set

D3885603-00

39

15/05/2025

User Manual – AB_AC/LPVT

TIme" (ID:57). Status signal related to fault indications is "Instant fault X" where X stands for R, S,
T or N line.
Example of Instantaneous Fault online R is shown below:
Figure 29 - Line R instant fault

“Voltage Absence Threshold” is a % of “Reference Voltage” (ID:31). The range of “Voltage
Absence Threshold” (ID:33) is 0.0~100.0% its setting unit is 0.1%.
“Voltage Presence Threshold is a % of “Reference Voltage” (ID:31). The range of “Voltage
Presence Threshold” (ID:32) is 0.0~100.0% its setting unit is 0.1%.
If ID:57 is 0, instantaneous fault detection is disabled.

Fault Inrush and Instantaneous Indications Reset

Fault inrush indications and fault instantaneous indications can be cleared and turn off light
indicators by the follow way:
•

External re-trigger: Command signal will be used to allow user clearing manually fault
notifications.

Table 8 – Inrush and instantaneous indications
Fault Type

Command to reset

Inrush

Fault. Inrush fault reset

Instantaneous

Fault. Instant fault reset

Fault Permanent Indications Reset

The parameter to be used for entering the configuration is “Permanent Fault Indicators Resetting
Mode” (ID:60).
There are three possible modes that can be configured to clear fault notifications and turn off light
indicators.

40

•

Fixed time: Fault notification will be cleared after a definite time. In case of selecting this
option, the ID:60 configuration menu ask for a time setting with 1min base time.

•

External re-trigger: Command signal will be used to allow user clearing manually fault
notifications.

Command to reset

Permanent

Fault. Sustained fault reset

•

Fault Restitution: Fault notifications will be reset when power supply is restored. Fault will
be considered as restored when a voltage value(s) on a R, S, or T phases becomes higher
than a “Voltage Presence Threshold” (ID:32) during a time higher than “Voltage Presence
Time for Supply Recovery (ID:65)

Example of fault restitution is shown below:
Figure 30 - Fault restitution.

Several modes could be selected at the same time.

Fault Direction

Fault direction will be indicated when operating zone parameters are introduced. When fault is
detected downstream, "Direction line" will be indicated. In case of upstream fault, "Direction bars"
will be signaled.
The operating zone for each indication can be configured with the below parameters:
Table 10 - Fault direction
Fault Type
Neutral faults

Identifier
ID:51 Fault. Neutral Maximum Torque Angle (degrees)
ID:54 Fault. Neutral Operating Zone (degrees)

Phase faults

ID:52 Fault. Phase Maximum Torque Angle (degrees)
ID:55 Fault. Phase Operating Zone (degrees)

SEF Faults

ID:53 Fault. SEF Maximum Torque Angle (degrees)
ID:56 Fault. SEF Operating Zone (degrees)

For neutral faults, in order to detect the fault direction, neutral voltage must be greater than the
threshold indicated in ID:34. It has to be introduced as % of the nominal voltage.
An example when “Operating Zone” (ID:51, ID:52 or ID:53) is equal to 0 and “Maximum Torque
Angle” (ID:54, ID:55 or ID:56) is equal to 80 is shown below:

D3885603-00

41

15/05/2025

User Manual – AB_AC/LPVT

Figure 31 - Torque operating zone.

If Operation Zone = 0, do not see the direction.

Overcurrent

AB_AC LPVT will indicate the presence of an over current on the line when a current value(s).
becomes higher than a set value (Phase Overcurrent Set Value ID:67 | Neutral Overcurrent Set
Value ID:69) during a set time (Phase Overcurrent Set Time ID:68 | Neutral Overcurrent Set Value
ID:70), “Overcurrent X” will be set on the status indicators. X can be R, S, T or N lines.
The range of the “Overcurrent set value” (Phase Overcurrent Set Value ID:67 | Neutral Overcurrent
Set Value ID:69) will be 0~ 840A and its setting unit is 1A. In case the setting value is “0”,
“Overcurrent X” events will not be set.
The range of “Overcurrent set time” (Phase Overcurrent Set Time ID:68 | Neutral Overcurrent Set
Value ID:70) will be 0~180000ms and its setting unit is 1ms.
“Overcurrent set value” and “Overcurrent set time” will be set respectively on both “Phase” and
“Neutral”.
Example of Overcurrent online R is shown below:
Figure 32 - Overcurrent line R.

If ID67 and ID69 = 0, the function is disabled.

Over Voltage

Overvoltage will be notified when R, S, or T lines becomes higher than a set value (Over Voltage
Set Value ID:71) during a set time (Over Voltage Set Time ID:72), “Over voltage X” will set on the
status indicators. X can be R, S or T lines.

“Over voltage Set Value (ID:71) is a multiple of “Reference Voltage” (ID:66). The range of the “Over
voltage Set Value” (ID:71) will be 0~1.5pu (“pu” is multiple of reference voltage ID:66) and its
setting unit is 0.01pu. In case the setting value is “0”, “Over voltage X” events will not be set.
The range of “Over voltage set time” (ID:72) will be 0~18000ms and its setting unit is 1ms.
Example of Over Voltage online R is shown below:

D3885603-00

51

15/05/2025

User Manual – AB_AC/LPVT

Figure 33 - Over voltage line R.

If ID71 = 0, the function is disabled.

Voltage Interruptions

A voltage value(s) on a R, S or T lines becomes lower than a set value (ID:73) during a set time.
Depending on the duration of the interruption “Momentary Interrupt X”, “Temporary Interrupt X” or
“Sustained Interrupt X” will be set on the status indicators. X can be R, S or T lines.
“Interrupt set value (ID:73) is a multiple of “Reference Voltage” (ID:66).The range of the
“Interruption set value” (ID:73) will be 0~0.49pu (“pu” is multiple of “Reference Voltage” (ID:66))
and its setting unit is 0.01pu. In case the setting value is “0”, “Interrupt X” events will not be set.
According to duration and IEEE std. 1159(PQM) classification, voltage interruptions are classified
as:
Table 17 - Voltage interruptions
Categories

Value

0~0.49 pu

0.01 pu

3 sec~1 min

0~0.49 pu

0.01 pu

More than1 min

0~0.49 pu

0.01 pu

Voltage Sags

A voltage value(s) on a R, S or T phases becomes lower than a set value (ID:74) “Sag max”, and
higher than a set value (ID:75) “Sag min” during a set time. Depending on the duration of the Sag,

52

D3885603-00

User Manual – AB_AC/LPVT

15/05/2025

“Instant Sag X”, “Momentary Sag X” or “Temporary Sag X” will be set on the status indicators. X
can be R, S or T phases.
The range of the “Sag min set value” (ID:74) and “Sag max set value” (ID:75) will be 0~0.99pu
(“pu” is multiple of “Reference Voltage” (ID:66)) and its setting unit is 0.01pu. In case the setting
value is “0”, “Sag X” events will not be set.
According to duration and IEEE std. 1159(PQM) classification, sags are classified as:

Voltage Swell

A voltage value on a R, S or T phases becomes higher than a set value (ID:76) during a set time.
Depending on the duration off the Swell “Instant Swell X”, “Momentary Swell X” or “Temporary
Swell X” will set on the status indicators. X can be R, S or T phases.
The range of the “Swell set value” (ID:76) will be 1.0~1.4pu (“pu‟ is multiple of “Reference Voltage”
(ID:66)) and its setting unit is 0.01pu. In case the setting value is “1”, “Swell X” events will not be
set.
According to swell duration and IEEE std. 1159(PQM) classification, swells are classified as:

Voltage Unbalance

In case voltage unbalance (Reverse voltage / Steady-state voltage) level exceeds a set value
(ID:77) during a set time (ID:78), “Voltage unbalance” will be set on the status indicators.
The range of “Voltage unbalance level” (ID:77) will be 0~100%, and its setting unit is 1%. In case
the setting value is “0”, “Voltage unbalance” event will not be set.
The range of “Voltage unbalance time” (ID:78) will be 100~60000ms, and its setting unit is 1ms.

Current Unbalance

In case current unbalance (Reverse current /Steady-state current) level exceeds a set value (ID:79)
during a set time (ID:80), “Current unbalance” will be set on the status indicators.
The range of “Current unbalance level” (ID:79) will be 0~100%, and its setting unit is 1%. In case
the setting value is “0”, “Current unbalance” event will not be set.
The range of “Current unbalance time” (ID:80) will be 100~60000ms, and its setting unit is 1ms.

Under Frequency

In case frequency value lower than a set value (Under Frequency Set Value ID:81) during a set
time (Under Frequency Set Time ID:82), “Under frequency” will be set on the monitored indicator.
The range of “Under frequency value” (ID:81) will be 0~59.98Hz, its setting unit is 0.01Hz. In case
the setting value is “0”, “Under frequency” event will not be set.
The range of “Under frequency time” (ID:82) will be 30~10000ms, and its setting unit is 1ms.

54

D3885603-00

User Manual – AB_AC/LPVT

15/05/2025

Figure 34 - Under frequency event detection.

Current THD

In case current THD higher than a set value (Current THD Set Value ID:83) during a set time
(Current THD Set Time ID:84), “Current THD phase X” will be set on the status indicators. X can be
R, S or T phases.
The range of “Current THD value” (ID:83) will be 0~100, its setting unit is 0.1. In case the setting
value is “0”, “Current THD phase X” will not be set.
The range of “Current THD time” (ID:84) will be 0~180000ms, and its setting unit is 1ms.

be R, S or T phases.
“Voltage THD Set Value” (ID:85) is a percentage of “Reference Voltage” (ID:66)). The range of
“Voltage THD value” (ID:85) will be 0~100, its setting unit is 0.1. In case the setting value is “0”,
“Voltage THD phase X” will not be set.

Phase Open

In case voltage value(s) become lower than voltage off level (Voltage Off Set Value ID:87) during
delay time (Phase Open Delay Time ID:89), “Phase open X” will be set on status indicators. X can
be R, S or T lines.
The range of “Voltage off level” (ID:87) will be 0.0~0.75pu (“pu” is multiple of “Reference Voltage”
(ID:66)), its setting unit is 0.05pu. If ID:87 is zero, Phase Open is deactivated.

In case the voltage value(s) become higher than voltage on level (Voltage On Set Value ID:88)
during delay time (Phase Open Delay Time ID:89), the “Phase open X” will be reset.
The Range of “Voltage on level” is 0.7~0.85pu (“pu” is multiple of “Reference Voltage” (ID:66)), its
setting unit is 0.05pu.
The range of “Phase Open Delay Time” will be 0~180000ms, and its setting unit is 1ms.
Example of Phase Open online R is shown below:

56

D3885603-00

User Manual – AB_AC/LPVT

15/05/2025

Figure 35 - Phase open indication process line R.

Under Voltage

In case voltage value(s) on R, S or T phases becomes lower than a set value (Under Voltage Set
Value ID:90) during a set time (Under Voltage Set Time ID:91), “Under voltage X” will be set on the
status indicators. X can be R, S or T lines.

The range of “Under voltage set value” (ID:90) will be 0~0.95pu (“pu” is multiple of “Reference
Voltage” (ID:66)) and its setting unit is 0.01pu. In case the setting value is “0”, “Under voltage X”
events will not be set.
The range of “Under Voltage Set Time” (ID:91) shall be 0~180000ms, and its setting unit is 1ms.

If more than one error is detected for a signal, the hexadecimal value monitored for quality bits will
result from the sum of all. For example:
0x00000001 indicates that an overflow has occurred and 0x00000002 indicates that there has
been a carry on a counter or a roll-over. The value 0x00000003 would appear monitored.

Firmware Upgrade
Once the ITB is configured, the HU module already knows the type of acquisition blocks that are
been installed and their corresponding addresses.
To update the software of the AB_AC/LPVT module, you need:
•

A console connection with the HU.

•

AB_AC/LPVT module must be correctly addressed and working fine in the ITB (LED Run must
be blinking).

•

AB_AC2.bin file with the new version. This file must be available in the flash folder of the HU. If
not, you can contact Schneider Electric in order to ask for it. Use an SFTP client and transfer
this binary file into the HU flash folder.

The console tool can be executed using any commercial tool for serial or SSH connection. In this
manual, PuTTy software is used.
Open a serial connection as follow:
Figure 45 – Putty configuration.

SSH connection via Ethernet can be used too. More information about the console use in the HU
user manual. In the following picture, HUe is used as CPU:
Figure 46 – SSH Connection.

64

D3885603-00

User Manual – AB_AC/LPVT

15/05/2025

It is necessary to login in the console with INSTALLER role user. No other user has permissions to
execute this operation. Please, consult the HU user manual for more information.
Once logged with INSTALLER role user, the BLSHELL is shown:

If the file AB_AC2.bin is available in the flash folder, use one of the following commands to upgrade
the software of AB_AC/LPVT modules:
Table 27 – Upgrading AB_AC/LPVT’s software.
Module

claqUpgrade “all”

Console commands are case-sensitive, and quotation marks must be included.
After running the command, the console will show some messages indicating the process status.
Here below it is shown an example of how the firmware of an AB_MIO module is updated. It is
similar for AB_AC/LPVT:

When you run the command, the first step is to check the version that is installed in the module.
After that, the file transfer to the module begins.
Finally, if the process has finished successfully, the console will show information about the
process as well as an upgrading summary, as shown here below.

Calibration
The AB_AC LPVT module is calibrated in factory with dedicated, high precision calibration
equipment. Under normal circumstances it will not be necessary to (re)calibrate the equipment.
(Re)calibrate is not recommended since the factory calibration values are lost in the (re)calibration
process.
If really required, signal calibration can be performed in the field with the ITB running in data
acquisition mode. Except for the module under calibration, all the remaining acquisition blocks in
the ITB will continue operating normally during this operation.
AB_AC LPVT module calibration must be performed with a calibrated three-phase generator.
The calibration process will be performed with webApp.
If the user-performed calibration is lost for any particular reason, the LED Mnt will switch on and the
module will need to be recalibrated for proper operation.

Wiring for Calibration
To calibrate the voltage and currents inputs the wiring is as follows:

Glossary
A
A: Ampere. Base unit of electric current in the International System of Units.
AAP: Automatic Addressing Procedure.
AB: EasyLogic T150 Acquisition Block.
AB_AI: Acquisition block for analog inputs.
AB_AO: Acquisition block for analog outputs.
AB_AC/LPVT: Acquisition block for digital inputs.
AB_AC/LPVTDO: Acquisition block for digital inputs and outputs.
AB_DO: Acquisition block for digital outputs.
AB_SER: Acquisition block for serial communications.
AC: Alternate Current.
AI: Analog Input.
AO: Analog Output.
AWG: American Wire Gauge.

C
ºC: Celsius degrees.
EMC: ElectroMagnetic Compatibility.
CPU: Central Processing Unit.

D
DC: Direct Current.
DI: Digital Input.
DIN: Deutsches Institut für Normung..
DO: Digital Output.

E
EEPROM: Electrically Erasable PROgrammable read-only Memory.
EOL: End-of-Life. According to the WEEE CE Directive, the EOL document provides instructions
for discard the product at the end of its life.

G
G: Grame.
GPS: Global Positioning System.

H
HU: Head Unit.

Important Information
Read these instructions carefully and look at the equipment to become familiar with the device
before trying to install, operate, service, or maintain it. The following special messages may appear
throughout this documentation or on the equipment to warn of potential hazards or to call attention
to information that clarifies or simplifies a procedure.
The addition of this symbol to a “Danger” or “Warning” safety label indicates that an
electrical hazard exists which will result in personal injury if the instructions are not
followed.

This is the safety alert symbol. It is used to alert you to potential personal injury
hazards. Obey all safety messages that follow this symbol to avoid possible injury
or death.

! DANGER

!

DANGER indicates an imminently hazardous situation which, if not avoided, will result in death or
serious injury.

!

WARNING

WARNING indicates a potentially hazardous situation which, if not avoided, can result in death or
serious injury.

!

CAUTION
NOTICE

Please Note
Electrical equipment must be installed, operated, serviced, and maintained only by qualified
personnel. No responsibility is assumed by Schneider Electric for any consequences arising out of
the use of this material.

A qualified person is one who has skills and knowledge related to the construction and operation of
electrical equipment and its installation and has received safety training to recognize and avoid the
hazards involved.

Safety Instructions
DANGER
HAZARD OF ELECTRIC SHOCK, EXPLOSION, OR ARC FLASH
•

Apply appropriate personal protective equipment (PPE) and follow
safe electrical work practices. See NFPA 70E, NOM-029-STPS2011, CSA Z462, NFC 18–510 and UTE C18–510–1.

•

This unit must be installed and serviced only by qualified
electrical personnel.

•

Turn off all power supplies of this unit before working on or inside the
unit.

•

Always use a properly rated voltage sensing device to confirm that
power is off.

•

To open a live current transformer secondary circuit, turn off the
primary side of the transformer and short-circuit the transformer
secondary circuit.

•

Set all devices, doors, and covers before turning on power to this unit.

Failure to follow these instructions will result in death or serious injury.

General Cybersecurity Information
In the recent years, the growing number of networked machines and production plants has seen a
corresponding increase in the potential for cyber threats, such as unauthorized access, data
breaches, and operational disruptions. You must, therefore, consider all possible cybersecurity
measures to help protect assets and systems against such threats.
To help keep your Schneider Electric products secure and protected, it is in your best interest to
implement the cybersecurity best practices as described in the Cybersecurity Best Practices
document.
Schneider Electric provides additional information and assistance:
• Subscribe to the Schneider Electric security newsletter.
• Visit the Cybersecurity Support Portal web page to:
◦ Find Security Notifications.
◦ Report vulnerabilities and incidents.
• Visit the Schneider Electric Cybersecurity and Data Protection Posture web
page to:
◦ Access the cybersecurity posture.
◦ Learn more about cybersecurity in the cybersecurity academy.
◦ Explore the cybersecurity services from Schneider Electric.

Product Related Cybersecurity Information
Cyber threats are part of the digital world of communicating products. The EasyLogic T150 Hue
module is concerned about possible cyber security issues and offers features to users to manage
the Cyber security and reduce the risks.

!

WARNING

HAZARD OF POTENTIAL COMPROMISE OF SYSTEM AVAILABILITY,
INTEGRITY, SAFETY, AND CONFIDENTIALITY
•

Change default passwords/passcodes to help prevent unauthorized
access to device settings and information. Implement strong
authentication controls, with high complexity passwords.

•

Disable unused ports/services and default accounts where possible, to
minimize pathways for malicious attacks.

•

Place networked devices behind multiple layers of cyber defences
(such as firewall, network segmentation, and network intrusion
detection and protection).

•

Use cybersecurity best practices (for example: least privilege,
separation of duties) to help prevent unauthorized exposure, loss,
modification of data and logs, interruption of services, unintended
operation, or potentially damaging the infrastructure or injuring
operating people.

Failure to follow these instructions can result in death, serious injury,
or equipment damage.
NOTE: In the event of losing access to the device, the device must be returned
to your respective Schneider Electric branch office.

8

D3885557-00

User Manual – EasyLogic T150 Platform

15/04/2025

Potential Risks and Compensating Controls
NOTICE
HAZARD OF UNAUTHORIZED ACCESS
Always uses protective access functionality provided by the product to
avoid cyber threads.
Failure to follow these instructions can result in equipment damage.
•

Insecure protocols

Modbus (Serial and TCP), IEC 60870-5-101, IEC60870-5-104 client, IEC 61850 (IEC 61850 MMS
(Manufacturing Message Specification) and IEC 61850 GOOSE (Generic Object-Oriented System
Event)) protocols, and some IT protocols (SNTP, DHCP, GPS) are insecure. If a malicious user
gains access to your network, transmitted information could be disclosed or subject to tampering.
List of protocols with security authentication capabilities are:
o
o

IEC 60870-5-104 server
DNP3 client and server

To transmit data over an internal network, physically or logically segment the network and restrict
access using standard controls such as firewalls and in particular IP Table allowlists.
•

Enclosure

The physical integrity of the installation must be ensured by the end user. Physical access to the
EasyLogic T150 modules and the operational area must be restricted.
•

Port hardening

Schneider Electric recommends carrying out the following actions:
•
•

Firewall: use the IP allowlist to limit external accesses to known devices.
Port configuration: disable unused physical ports.

Contact your SE branch office or field service representative for more information about cyber
security features on the EasyLogic T150 platform.
NOTE: Schneider Electric regularly posts security notifications with information on vulnerabilities
and patches that it receives from its partners at the U.S. Department of Homeland Security’s ICSCERT and United States Computer Emergency Readiness Team (US-CERT), other ISACs, and
cybersecurity firms, among others. These updates are designed to fix known vulnerabilities and are
encouraged for any Internet-connected device. You can also subscribe to our newsletter to receive
security notifications.

NOTICE
HAZARD OF CYBERSECURITY BREACH
Report a cybersecurity matter on a Schneider Electric product or solution via the Report a
cybersecurity vulnerability page.
Failure to follow these instructions can result in equipment damage.
